name: Digital delivery of social protection entitlements
description: Interventions and research examining how technology mediates the delivery of welfare scheme benefits (rations, pensions, subsidies) to citizens, particularly in rural India. This domain spans the design flaws, exclusion errors, and accountability gaps that emerge when authentication and payment systems are inserted into entitlement delivery pipelines, and it intersects ethnographic critique, impact evaluation, and operational scale.

name: Technology-assisted grievance redress and citizen engagement in welfare programs
description: Systems and models that build feedback loops, grievance channels, and transparency mechanisms into the implementation of government welfare schemes, often via civil-society intermediaries or automated platforms. This domain covers how citizens surface and resolve exclusion/complaints, blending sociotechnical design, participatory engagement, and evaluation of redress outcomes.

name: Satellite imagery and geospatial ML for governance and monitoring
description: Use of remote sensing and machine learning on satellite/geospatial data to detect regulatory compliance (e.g., brick kilns) or predict socio-economic indicators at scale for policy and administrative purposes. This domain connects CS methods with impact evaluation and operational deployment for environmental and welfare monitoring.
